title: Senecio cadiscus
intro: Senecio cadiscus is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae), commonly known as a member of the ragwort genus. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it traditionally used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Senecio cadiscus include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in ointments and salves to take advantage of its topical anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.
taxonomy: Senecio cadiscus is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Senecio, which is a group of plants commonly known as ragworts or groundsels. The specific classification of Senecio cadiscus is still being researched, but it is generally considered to be a member of the Senecio genus within the Asteraceae family. Further research is needed to determine the exact classification and relationships of this species. The Senecio cadiscus is native to the southwestern Cape Province of South Africa.
